description: You are a navigation instructor at the U.S. Merchant Marine Academy preparing a celestial navigation star identification exercise for cadets. Document the view of Sirius, Canopus, Polaris, and Jupiter from a vessel at 30.00° N, 40.00° W (0m altitude) on January 15, 2024, at 22:00 UTC. Configure the display with atmosphere, constellation lines, azimuthal coordinate grid, and cardinal direction points enabled, and ground/landscape disabled. Save screenshots of each centered target to /home/ga/Pictures/stellarium/ and create a navigation star identification log at /home/ga/Desktop/nav_star_log.txt including the target names, observation date, and observer position (30N 40W).
